titleBlock.headline: Inhibition of IL-11 Signalling Extends Mammalian Healthspan and Lifespan
titleBlock.authors: Widjaja, A.A., Cook, S.A., et al.

researchSummary:
This is a summary of the scientific study.
Not affiliated with the study authors or their institutions.
whatIsIL11: Interleukin-11 (IL-11) is a pro-inflammatory cytokine. While historically known for its role in platelet formation, it is now recognized as a major driver of fibrosis and inflammation.
Why does it matter for aging?

As mammals age, IL-11 levels progressively increase across multiple tissues. This chronic elevation acts as a master switch, driving the hallmarks of aging including cellular senescence, mitochondrial dysfunction, and tissue fibrosis.

By genetically deleting IL-11 or administering an anti-IL-11 neutralizing antibody to older mice, researchers achieved a staggering ~25% extension in median lifespan alongside profound improvements in healthspan.

studyOverview.label: Objective
studyOverview.value: Determine if inhibiting IL-11 signaling can delay aging and extend lifespan in mice.

studyOverview.label: Design
studyOverview.value: Genetic deletion models and pharmacological intervention (anti-IL-11 antibody) in aged mice.

studyOverview.label: Lifespan Result
studyOverview.value: Median lifespan increased by 22.5% in males and 25% in females.

studyOverview.label: Healthspan Result
studyOverview.value: Reversal of frailty, improved metabolic function, and preserved muscle mass.

mechanismDescription: IL-11 acts as a critical upstream regulator of the aging process. Its age-induced upregulation triggers a pathological signaling cascade that suppresses nutrient sensing and drives cellular senescence.

pathwaySteps.desc: IL-11 expression chronically increases in tissues as mammals age.
IL-11 binds to IL-11RA, initiating pathological intracellular signaling.
The ERK pathway is hyperactivated, AMPK is suppressed, and mTORC1 becomes hyperactive.
This drives cellular senescence, mitochondrial dysfunction, and widespread tissue fibrosis.

keyFindings.title: Massive Lifespan Extension
keyFindings.desc: Anti-IL-11 antibody treatment in 75-week-old mice (equivalent to 55-year-old humans) extended median lifespan by up to 25%, making it one of the most effective pharmacological longevity interventions ever discovered.

keyFindings.title: Reversal of Physical Frailty
keyFindings.desc: Treated mice exhibited significantly improved grip strength, better motor coordination, and a profound reduction in clinical frailty scores compared to controls.

keyFindings.title: Metabolic Rejuvenation
keyFindings.desc: Inhibition of IL-11 reduced age-associated white adipose tissue accumulation, preserved lean muscle mass, and restored youthful glucose metabolism and lipid profiles.

keyFindings.title: Cellular Senescence Reduction
keyFindings.desc: The therapy systemically lowered biomarkers of cellular senescence, effectively acting as a senomorphic intervention to suppress the SASP (Senescence-Associated Secretory Phenotype).

conclusions: IL-11 is a fundamental, targetable driver of mammalian aging.
Blocking IL-11 signaling safely extends both healthspan and lifespan in preclinical models.
Anti-IL-11 therapies represent a highly promising, near-term clinical approach to treating age-related multimorbidity in humans.
protocolRecommendations: IL-11 inhibition represents a cutting-edge pharmacological frontier. While currently in clinical trials for fibrotic diseases, it is not yet available as a longevity therapeutic.
In the interim, lifestyle interventions that reduce systemic inflammation and suppress the ERK/mTORC1 axis (like endurance exercise and fasting) remain the most effective accessible strategies.
